But, it is highly unlikely.

This is particularly due to the way the convertible notes are structured and multiple price variables at play.

It would need a "mayday" situation to occur.

Now, let's take a look at the credit agreements on this debt.

Just about the only way a "forced liquidation" occurs if there is a "fundamental change" at the company.

They have a leverage ratio of ~19%.

As seen below, MOST of this debt is held in convertible notes with conversion prices BELOW the current share price.

Most of this doesn't mature until 2028. https://t.co/JZdps65ooQ

First, $MSTR's strategy is largely contingent on the ability to raise additional capital.

In a situation where their liabilities rise significantly higher than their assets, this ability could deteriorate.

However, this doesn't necessarily mean "forced liquidation." https://t.co/jWxQT8m5kD

$MSTR has been buying Bitcoin for years and there have been MULTIPLE bear markets since then.

$MSTR - MICROSTRATEGY: DIDN'T SELL SHARES OR BUY BITCOIN LAST WEEK

「#BTC 上昇サイクル未終了、上昇余力を示唆」 
ビットコインが1月に109,000ドルという史上最高値を記録した後、現在97,000ドル台で安定しています。技術的な調整の可能性はあるものの、半減期サイクルはまだ終わっていません。

半減期サイクルの評価 
2020年のビットコインの半減期とその後のスポット価格のピークを見ると、2020年5月11日から2021年11月8日までの間に、資産価値は合計686%上昇しました。 一方、2024年4月20日の最新の半減期から最近の史上最高値まで、ビットコインは63%しか上昇していません。 ビットコインサイクルの進行における累乗則モデルと利回りの低下を考慮すると、今後はより緩やかな価格上昇が予想されますが、過去のサイクルと比較すると、63%の上昇はまだ上昇余地があることを示しています。

先行指標としての$MSTR 
MicroStrategy(MSTR、旧MicroStrategy)は2025年初頭にもビットコインを買い続け、最近では7,633BTCを追加購入しました。 (現在、合計478,740BTCを保有しています) MSTRのビットコイン購入は、機関投資家の需要を反映しており、価格動向の先行指標として機能します。Strategyの買い越しは概ね市場の流れと一致しており、買い越しが多ければ多いほど、現物価格の上昇を示唆しています。逆に、MSTRの買いが減れば、弱気相場を意味する可能性があります。

今後の見通し 
半減期サイクルが続いているにもかかわらず、「5月に売り、5月に買い」という格言があるように、上半期の下落、夏の横ばい、そして年末の価格上昇が予想されます。これは、第4四半期に大きな上昇が見られたこと（2013年、2016年、2017年、2020年、2020年、2021年、2023年、2024年に繰り返された）に基づいています。 ただし、ウクライナ戦争の停戦などのマクロ的なイベントは、ビットコインを含むリスク資産の価値を大きく上昇させる要因となる可能性があります。

(Very long post, trigger warning)

First, what is a preferred stock? If you want a good primer, here's a @PrestonPysh video from 12 years ago going through the basics: https://t.co/862SIn9Ydm 

So what is a 'Perpetual' preferred stock, and what might it mean for $MSTR?? 

TLDR: A perpetual preferred stock is a hybrid financial instrument that combines features of debt and equity, offering fixed dividend payments with no maturity date. It sits between debt and common stock on a company's capital structure, meaning preferred shareholders are paid dividends before common shareholders (no dividends for common shareholders in this case) but after debt holders. 

In liquidation, they also have priority over common stockholders. While preferred shareholders usually lack voting rights, they benefit from a more stable income stream and greater security in dividend payments. This structure allows companies to raise capital without diluting voting control (!!!!!), while offering investors a balance of income and relative safety.

The most important thing to understand is that as the preferred stock is perpetual, there is no lump sum principal repayment. There will be an annual dividend, and depending on the rate it is issued at, it is equivalent to pulling forward 10-20 years of buying power. 

At a 5% rate, you are essentially pulling forward 20 years of cash. 

So lets dive into the profile of preferreds: 

Preferred stocks are low volatility, and provide less upside than common equity typically.  Banks, Utility companies, REITs, etc. 

For some numbers:

Globally, there is 3,488 issues of preferred stock worth over $1m outstanding. On average, $555m per issue, worth approximately $1.93 trillion in aggregate.

More specifically, if we narrow it down to parameters that match where $MSTR likely targets, there are currently 306 securities that check the following boxes: 

- Exchange listed 
- Perpetual 
- U.S. listed issuer 

- $378m avg. value per issue 
- $115.69 billion in aggregate across 306 issues
- Avg. initial dividend rate of 6.283% 

So who is the target buyer for preferred stock? 
- Insurance companies  
- Pension funds 
- Mutual funds
- Banks

Notably, preferred stock can be held as Tier 1 or Tier 2 capital on bank balance sheets under Basel III banking regulations. 

Preferred stocks are low volatility, low performance, income generating securities. Shown below is the 60-day historical volatility of a preferred stock index. 10% volatility, or about 1/10th of $MSTR currently. 

This will be the first time we are seeing what falls under the '$MSTR Dividend*' category that MicroStrategy teased on its Q3 earnings presentation.  

Slide 29: https://t.co/FDkL5lIv9l

However, there is an extremely important advantage that $MSTR has compared to nearly all other preferred issuers: 

Volatility. (Not to mention subsequent common stock liquidity,  performance, and $BTC exposure)

"The perpetual preferred stock may include features such as (i) convertibility to our class A common stock." - MSTR Preferred Stock filing https://t.co/MuXKBTFXOa

What $MSTR can provide to fixed income investors is an indefinite $BTC backed call option 

Let's do some option math: 

The value of MSTR calls options with the market implied vol (87%), based on a reference price of $332 per share of $MSTR : 

+50% out of the money:  
- 1-year expiry: $89.56 (26.36% of price today) 
- 5-year expiry: $222.13 (65.4%) 
- 10-year expiry: $286.28 (84.28%)
- 20-year expiry: $326.42 (96.10%)

Now, let's do something much MUCH further out of the money, just for the sake of the example. 

The value of +500% (!) out of the money calls options with the market implied vol (87%), based on a reference price of $332 per share of $MSTR :

- 1-year expiry: $20.93 (6.16% of price today)
- 5-year expiry: $140.97 (41.50%)
- 10-year expiry: $243.16 (71.59%)
- 20-year expiry: $314.73 (92.66%)

Are you starting to understand how valuable an indefinite call option on a ~90% volatility $BTC beta stock can be for fixed income investors??  

The right tail 'risk" is massive for an indefinite call option on a hyper-volatile stock. 

The path forward for Saylor and Co. is selling volatility, this time, to preferred stock investors. It's not debt that has to be paid down outside of the annual dividend, and even if we generously assume they pay an average preferred rate of ~6% (I would bet they fetch below average), that would only be $120m per year in dividends on a $2 billion raise, for a company that raised > $15b in equity capital alone in 2024. Non-issue. 

We have no idea what the pricing will look like i.e. where $MSTR will place the strike, or what the initial dividend rate will be, so we are just sort of approximating for now.

I can't see $MSTR issuing preferred without a conversion option at some ratio to common stock. After-all, volatility is THE product, and BTC Yield is the Key Performance Indicator. The indefinite optionality is THE most interesting product that MSTR can sell to the fixed income market. 

Thus, given the immense theoretical value of the imbedded indefinite call, it might make sense to apply a conversion ratio less than 1.0 for every preferred share to common. 

Something like 0.50, or 0.20 conversion into common for every share of preferred for a conversion ratio (i.e 2 or 5 shares of preferred stock convert into 1 share of common stock) could make sense;  but again, we can't know until its been priced and placed. 

But what we do know is how much Saylor and team emphasize the value of volatility. 

"Volatility is vitality."

The treasury team has spoken at length about selling volatility (only through convert issues thus far) and recycling the proceeds back into BTC. 
 
This selling of volatility is how they take the preferred market by storm. 

Using our example from above, if an $MSTR call option was striked +500% out of the money (approx $2000/share currently), with 5 shares of preferred converting to 1 share of common stock, the theoretical call option in each preferred share (with a 10-year expiry just for the sake of the math) would be $48.63 of value. For just the option, nevermind the dividend paying component of the preferred share (!!!!). Now, instead of 10 years make it indefinite...

The most interesting thing to watch for will be the provisions that the conversion contains in the preferred offering. 

In the past, when $MSTR has issued convertible bonds, the bond has contained all sorts of provisions for the convertible component; a put option imbedded for the buyer of the debt, a call option that $MSTR can redeem if the bonds rise past a certain value after a period of time, etc. I posted some of that theoretical math here: https://t.co/4g9KwIN6Ds

The point of all these provisions is to essentially cap the upside and the downside of the convertible option. 

The imbedded 5-6 year call option in the convertible debt is purposefully neutered, because the convert buyers aren't actually interested in the directional exposure. 

These convert arb desks are in the business of 'gamma-trading', as they look to delta hedge out their exposure almost instantly. Nevermind exposure for six years, not even six months, weeks or days. These buyers hedge exposure on day 1.

So, they neutralize the value of the call so as to target something like approx 50% delta, so for every $1 billion of bonds that are issued, the convert bond arbitrage desks will short sell something like $500m of equity to lock in immediate gains. 

But, the preferred stock could be a bit different. 

Given the indefinite nature of the option (read: PERPETUAL preferred), an uncapped call would hold EXTREME value, even if the call was striked laughably far out of the money, given the immense volatility and time value of the option (as laid out above).

Stepping back, the preffered market is just a relative larger scale than converts. For scale, the total size of the convert market in the United States for listed securities is 1,360 bonds worth $302 billion, compared to $572 billion in total for all preferred stock. 

However, narrowing the parameters to what $MSTR is looking for: a 0% coupon issue attractive to arb desks is a much smaller market. There is a total of $50 billion of zero coupon convertible debt for listed companies in the United States. $MSTR is already far and away the largest and most active issuer in this market. 

Time to level up to a larger pool of capital.

The market for preferred stock in the U.S. is large, and the allocator class is entirely different, with much deeper pockets.

A question to all preferred stock buyers by forced mandate: Would you like exposure to a random tranche of the thousandth largest regional bank, Utility companies, and/or REITs, or would you like low volatility, high sharpe ratio upside exposure to the world's preeminent Bitcoin Treasury Company? 

No comparison. 

So, what's it all mean? 

In essence, $BTC has infiltrated the fixed income market; and the dreaded uncertainty (read: volatility) that every smug TradFi allocator spent the past 15 years making fun of, is the very reason that these $MSTR preferreds will likely be the top performing preferred security in the market before long, just like the $MSTR converts and the common stock have been.

$MSTR bears have no idea what just hit them. 

Oh, and don't get me started on the potential BTC Yield here...

🗽 美國新生效的加密貨幣會計準為何是BTC一大利好？

美國財務會計準則委員會（FASB）在去年針對加密貨幣專門制定的會計準則，於本月 15 日正式生效。按照這一最新規則，今後美國持有加密貨幣的企業，將能夠按照市场公允價值（Fair Value），將其持有的比特幣等數位資產計入其公司財報中。

🤔首個加密貨幣會計準則上路後有什麼不同？

此前，美國政府並未針對加密貨幣制定特定的會計準則，來指導持有加密貨幣的公司在其資產負債表上，該如何記錄和衡量其持有的加密貨幣。因此一般來講，這些公司只能將加密貨幣歸類到無形資產（Intangible Assets），這樣做的後果是，在進行登記時，就算貨幣價格上漲也只能以其「買入時的價格」來計算成本。

同時對於這些公司而言，其資產負債表上持有的加密貨幣如果價格下跌時只能進行減值；只有當決定賣出加密貨幣時，才能以獲利的方式進行資產登記。

“也就是說即使你買入比特幣的價格在 9 萬美元，即便上漲到 10 萬也只能以 9 萬美元計算。但是如果下跌到  8 萬美元時，就必須登記減值。”

作為世界上持有比特幣數量最多的上市公司微策略，就曾經向美國證券交易委員會（SEC）抱怨，這樣的會計準則對於公司和投資來說都不是一件好事，因為只允許減值的會計準則在財報上，無法讓董事會和投資者全面評估加密貨幣投資。

不過現在隨著新的會計準則上路，企業開始能夠以特別項目的分類，將持有的加密貨幣在資產負債表上單獨記錄，同時以公允價值來計算的話，這些公司也能將幣價上漲所帶來的收益計入其淨利，這對於微策略等投資比特幣或是以太坊的公司而言，在財報上都可以更為精準地呈現該公司的加密貨幣投資收入。

🚀MSTR 明年 6 月有望納入標普 500 指數

對此，Benchmark 股票分析師 Mark Palmer 17 日給客戶的報告提到，微策略計劃在 2025 年 Q1 採用 FASB，這將使該公司能立即將 BTC 公允價值的變動計入每期的淨收入：

“這意味著 MicroStrategy 將在 2025 年第一季度錄得一次性、多達數十億美元的季度淨收入增幅，這將反映其帳面比特幣價值與市場價值之間的差異。”

Palmer 補充表示，如果這筆收益足以抵銷過去三個季度的虧損，並且讓過去 12 個月的累計收益呈現正值，那麼微策略就有機會在 2025 年 6 月納入標普 500 指數。

隨著微策略已經納入那斯達克 100 指數，以及有望在 2025 年中旬納入標普 500 指數，這些因素都有助於該公司股票上漲。

而股票上漲對微策略未來繼續發行公司債是有利的行為，因此可以推測該公司未來繼續買入比特幣的機會將會持續更久的時間，有望持續推動比特幣價格繼續成長。

🧵第一个吃螃蟹的项目@solvprotocol

对于solv这个项目我是很早就参与了的，当然主要是因为先在merlin存了btc，solv部署后顺手就存进去了，预期只是一个理财收益，没有特别深入了解。

在这次solv抢先拍下hyperliquid席位后，我觉得有必要重新评估一下这个项目的质量，下面长文深入聊一下：

我已经详细解释过，hype目前的两大价值一个是合约价值捕获，第二是壳价值市场化。
另外，未来hypeEVM的价值是能让项目同时获得交易用户+产品用户。

1️⃣布局

投资就是投人，特别是币圈，币的价值偏向债而不是股，币更多衡量的是一个项目的偿债能力。
所以项目方的degen程度对项目的长期运营有重要影响，如果一个项目自己就是冤大头，各种被上下游剥削，你还指望他带你赚钱？
记住，羊毛出在羊身上，不必要的高额上币费最终都是剥削社区份额或者让市场来买单。

solv率先以15万美元果断拍下席位，这个费用昨天已经被拉到48万，显然，未来还会继续提升。
也是目前千万级融资/Binance投资项目中最早的，说实话我都没想到会这么快，这份远见足以说明这个项目的运营思路非常清晰。
加上solv今年all in btc生态站在风口，这种决策力，整体布局看下来还是比较靠谱的。

2️⃣配合

一个项目是想找点退出流动性出个货结束，还是想好好做下去，也是你能不能格局并赚到大钱的关键，hype已经证明了好好做产品的意义，而solv这种多链流动性协议其实是最适合在hyperliquid上发币的。

因为如果你是一条公链，特别是layer1，你的产品是链本身，那肯定没法在hyper EVM上部署，而LSD协议则没有这个问题，能将solvBTC引入Hyper，要知道hype上每天有20亿的BTC合约成交量。

此外还能享受到hyperliquid上最优质的流动性，目前hl上有20多亿usdc的清洁沉资，另外还有价值88亿美元的hype.

我不知道solv主力准备在hl上部署多少做市成本，也不知道未来solv是否有想法做hype质押的lsd资产，但如果你们有看到，确实可以好好考虑一下，btc的市场竞争非常激烈，solv拿下25000btc tvl已然是很成功，而hype的lsd市场还是尚待开发的处女地。

我知道或许最开始的决策里，拍下hyperliquid席位仅仅是多个cex 同步tge里性价比较高的一个，但如果在这个生态里继续build下去，很可能会有更大的惊喜，或许会是一段互相成就的佳话，未来Hyperliquid第一个顶级项目这个标签可能会大于Binance投资这些传统背景亮点。

3️⃣估值

solv这个项目本身怎么样呢？

我觉得solv跟hyperliquid在赛道对比这点上还是有点像的。
hyperliquid经常被拿来和其他perp dex或layer比，但hl的做的事是剑指cex.

同样的，作为链上btc储备龙头，solv经常被拿来跟其他lsd/cedefi项目去比，然而solv想做的是链上微策略。

微策略（MicroStrategy）这几年通过举债大量购入BTC，已持有386,700枚BTC，市值达到1000亿美元，在川皇登基前夕也是顺应天意，被收入了纳斯达克100指数。
微策略激进的用杠杆不断加仓比特币，使 $MSTR 成为最大的比特币机构持有者，自2020年以来，股价飙升了35倍，远超比特币本身的涨幅。

目前各种机构不断买入BTC，已经占据了14%的BTC份额，而这部分资金基本是仅托管的，只能发挥BTC的储值价值。

Solv在链上持有25K BTC，在BTC ETF中能排到第七，要知道ETF要收管理费，而Solv的五十几万用户可以选择4种产品让BTC产生额外收益，目前收益率在4.6～26%，利用率高达85%，以5%保守估计，每年能产生1.25亿收入，这部分如果再次流入BTC，将形成链上微策略的飞轮。

另外，对于风险敞口更大的用户，SolvBTC也可以在链上作为抵押物使用，对于微策略来说需要可转债/股票/币三者互相转化，最终可以实现的效果，solv可以无许可的帮助全部用户使用，进一步释放场内购买力。

如果按微策略的市值来等比估算，slov的fdv估值能达到64亿美元，再算上创造收益的部分，这个数字可能会进一步提高。

5️⃣展望

未来solv的发展空间，他们看起来只想深耕btc，所以hype的lsd资产或许八字还没一撇，但BTC市场我觉得还是有空间。

增长点不仅是c端市场，目前b端也持有大量的BTC，比如cex，各种机构等等，要是能为他们提供更好的解决方案，solv或许会扶摇直上。

毕竟如果btc一直死拿不卖，那就相当于空气，人都是要死的，不能成为币的奴隶。
如果手上的BTC成为生息资产，那么越拿就会越有价值，也就无需考虑止盈，只要不断增加储备就可以了。

$MSTR Monthly Analysis 📈  

Trigger: 400 ✅ 
Trade idea: MSTR Dec 20 440C
Targets: 447,465 🎯 

Technical Setup:
$MSTR broke out of its long base in February. After consolidating for 7 months, it saw a massive breakout in October as Bitcoin broke its ATHs.  

Currently, $BTC is sitting just under $100K, with a recent pullback to $90K earlier this week. Similarly, $MSTR pulled back to $330 from its highs of $543.  

The $330 level is significant as it marks the highs from the Dot-com bubble. If $BTC pushes above $100K, we could see $MSTR’s next leg higher towards $700.  

Bitcoin Holdings:
MicroStrategy owns 386,700 BTC (as of Nov. 25, 2024), acquired at an average price of $56,658 per BTC, for a total cost of $21.91B. At current Bitcoin prices (~$94K), their holdings are worth over $36 billion.  
  
This strategy allows $MSTR to act as a leveraged proxy for Bitcoin, amplifying its value for investors during BTC bull runs.  

Valuation & Leverage:
The current market cap of $MSTR is $89.6B, with their BTC reserves accounting for ~40% of that value. Using a 2.5x multiple of their Bitcoin reserves, the company’s value mirrors a leveraged Bitcoin position.  
  
For every $10K increase in BTC, $MSTR’s BTC reserves grow by $3.867B. Applying the 2.5x multiple translates to a $10B increase in market cap which equates to approximately $44.50 per share.  

Price Target Scenarios:
If BTC reaches $200K by the end of 2025, $MSTR’s valuation under the 2.5x multiple should lead to a stock price near $850.  
 
My price target for $MSTR by the end of 2025 is $700, assuming BTC trades at/near $165K.  

Not financial advice – just my thoughts!

关于$mstr 的外部风险，mstr通过增发股票和发行可转债卖大饼的机制有很多详细介绍，@highFree2028 的这篇推文https://t.co/lmE22XE35y就很全面深入。今天华尔街日报这篇报道揭示$mstr 股票暴涨的另一个原因就更值得重视。个人的总结：
1、$mstr 的可转债打开了很多传统只能投资债券的大资金例如保险资金获取btc上涨收益多可能性，所以收到了追捧。
而$mstr 的股票也是很多资管机构能绕开无法直接购买大饼或者比特币etf的限制（要么监管要求，要么购买新的资产类别需要LP同意流程很长）的最优选择，$mstr 作为btc上涨的连接器受到了这些子资管机构热捧。

2、除了上面正常原因，三季度以来mstr得暴涨还有一个重要推手就是单一股票的高杠杆ETF基金。Defiance Daily Target 2X Long MSTR ETF 和 T-Rex 2X Long MSTR Daily Target ETF 是为那些希望在mstr股票上做出更激进押注的投资者设计的。自 8 月和 9 月分别推出以来这两只基金的资产总额已经增长到50 亿美元。对于一家市值900亿美金的上市公司来说、50亿美金的净资金流入对于股价的推动作用是巨大的。

3、杠杆 ETF 通常通过使用掉期来实现预期效果，这种掉期对于最大、最具流动性的股票来说是广泛可得的。掉期合约的支付与基础资产的表现直接挂钩，使基金能够精准地将股票或指数的每日表现放大一倍。

Matt Tuttle，Tuttle Capital 和 Rex Shares 2 倍杠杆 MicroStrategy 基金的经理表示，他无法获得所需的足够掉期来支撑其快速增长的基金。他说，他的主要经纪商目前提供给他的掉期额度为 2000 万到 5000 万美元，而上周某个时候他本可以使用 13 亿美元的掉期。基本上就是说，这些mstr杠杆etf基金已经难以实现2倍的回报了。

4、这些基金通过掉期和期权去进行杠杆操作，一旦表现不如预期，投资者对基金的偏差表现感到失望，批评者警告或者撤离资金，这些行为加剧了 MicroStrategy 股价的波动。

总结下来，mstr股价风险并不在内部（零息或者低息发行可转债风险可控），风险可能是在外部（单一股票杠杆etf带来的巨大波动）

Many claim that when buying $MSTR, you are effectively paying a "premium" to buy Bitcoin.

Saylor says that is like saying oil companies should only trade based on their oil reserves.

Just as oil needs to be refined into gasoline, he says $MSTR does the same for Bitcoin.

Now, let's take a look at options pricing on $MSTR.

The furthest possible expiration date on $MSTR $670 calls is January 2027, trading at ~$230.

That's a ~55% premium for calls that expire 2.5 years SOONER than the convertible bonds.

This makes the notes seem cheap. https://t.co/e7FZU2753b

With the convertible note offering, investors are effectively buying a call option on $MSTR with a $672 strike and 2029 expiration date.

The difficult part is computing what the zero coupon bond is actually worth.

Assume a large coupon of 10% and the call is priced at 38%. https://t.co/8ItvQ4HzJ6

In other words, the notes are priced at a 55% premium to $MSTR's share price.

This means that investors in these bonds ONLY get paid of $MSTR rises above $672.

At a high level, this appears to be a complete rip off, but we must compare this to pricing of options on the stock. https://t.co/4SD20Hj1aA

While several narratives have developed for the rationale behind the increasing $MSTR premium against its BTC holdings, I think there is a simpler explanation.

The first levered single stock ETF referencing MSTR began trading on August 15th.

On that date, MSTR closed at a market cap of $25.6 billion. BTC closed at $56.7k.

The two levered MSTR ETFs have since grown to $3.8 billion in AUM as of yesterday’s close. Adjusting for their 2x target exposures equals $7.7 billion in exposure. Against MSTR current market cap of $86.5 billion, this represents a remarkable 8.9% of the company’s total market cap.

In my opinion this has been a significant driving factor in MSTR’s outperformance. From August 15th through yesterday BTC is up 61%, while MSTR market cap is up 237%.

A question for TradFi folks: Knowing nothing else, what would you estimate the valuation to be for a company that has $20 billion of net equity and has repurchased $6.3 billion worth of stock so far in 2024?

Would you value the company at its net asset value of $20 billion? Probably not.

Now, let's imagine $MSTR had purchased zero additional BTC in 2024. An equivalent 26.4% year-to-date increase in BTC per fully diluted share could have been achieved through a buyback of 43.24 million shares.

With a 2024 $MSTR volume-weighted average price of $147.42, this would amount to $6.3 billion in stock buybacks.

But $MSTR hasn't been buying back shares; instead, it has issued equity and convertible notes to fund the purchase of an additional 90,270 BTC, leading to a 26.4% year-to-date increase in BTC per fully diluted share in 2024.

Bitcoin-native value accretion.

Michael Saylor is the Egg Man. His latest announcement is that $MSTR will spend another $42 billion to buy #Bitcoin, funded by issuing $21 billion in debt and $21 billion in equity over the next three years. This reminds me of a joke I heard a long time ago.

A client calls his broker inquiring about egg futures and is quoted a price of 25 cents per contract. Having a hunch about the egg market, he buys 100 contracts. A week later, he calls his broker to get a quote. Pleased to learn that the price per contract has risen to 35 cents, he decides to buy another 1,000 contracts. A few days later, eager to check on the progress of his investment, he is amazed to learn that the price has now risen to 50 cents per contract, twice the price he paid for his original 100 contracts. Sensing a trend, he steps it up, this time buying 100,000 contracts. The next day, ecstatic to learn that the price per contract has now risen to 65 cents, he gets even more aggressive, buying 1,000,000 contracts. Sure enough, the following day, the price per contract rises to 95 cents, prompting him to order an additional million contracts. The day after that, as rising prices further validate his intuition, he buys yet another million contracts, this time paying $1.25.

The next day, with egg contracts trading at $1.75, he senses that the market has risen too far too fast, and places an order to sell 2,000,000 contracts. After a pregnant pause, his broker replies, "Sell to whom, you're the egg man!"

(2b) Tuesday: Daily Market Brief
MicroStrategy: $700M Note Offering for Debt Swap and Bitcoin Buy

#MicroStrategy ($MSTR) has announced plans to offer $700 million in convertible senior notes due 2028, subject to market conditions. The unsecured notes will pay semi-annual interest and be convertible into cash, MSTR class A common stock, or a combination thereof. The company intends to use the proceeds to redeem $500 million of its outstanding 6.125% Senior Secured Notes due 2028, with the remaining funds allocated for additional #bitcoin purchases and general corporate purposes. This move follows MicroStrategy's recent acquisition of 18,300 BTC worth over $1 billion. The firm has accumulated 244,800 BTC since 2020, valued at nearly $10 billion, with an average purchase price of $38,585 per coin. Upon redemption of the Senior Secured Notes, collateral including approximately 69,080 #BTC will be released. MicroStrategy's stock dropped 4.9% following the announcement. The offering is available only to qualified institutional buyers under Rule 144A.

Crypto Equities Are Outperforming Bitcoin By 2x

Most of the investor interest in cryptocurrencies has historically been concentrated in the digital tokens. Retail investors were first to the party, followed by family offices, corporations, and financial institutions. The big hope from many is that nation states will be the next big shoe to drop in the adoption curve.

At the same time that adoption was sequentially moving from small accounts to large accounts, capital allocators were also moving horizontally through the market as well. First, investors were buying bitcoin, then Ether, and now a plethora of other crypto assets that push them further out on the risk curve.

These are all digital tokens though. One of the areas that “crypto investors” seem to always leave as an afterthought is the public equities involved in the crypto market. For example, @alyssachoo_ from Bitwise pointed out this week that 2023 continues to be a good year for crypto equities:

- Coinbase $COIN: +263% YTD
- Riot Platforms $RIOT: +274% YTD
- Marathon Digital $MARA: +260% YTD
- Microstrategy $MSTR: +260% YTD
- Galaxy Digital $GLXY: +114% YTD

The first four companies on this list have more than doubled the return of bitcoin year-to-date. While true, these results are not necessarily reflected in the public narrative at the moment.

But the recent past results are not necessarily indicative of future performance. Some investors are bearish on the ability for a crypto-native firm like Coinbase to succeed against a litany of traditional financial firms competing for customers and assets. Maybe these bears are right, maybe they aren’t. Predicting the future is hard.

Put that debate aside for a second though. A narrative that is forming around Coinbase specifically is that the company’s stock price will hit $1,000 during the bull market. If you search Twitter/X for “$COIN $1000” you will see many different accounts all but predicting this milestone as an eventuality. Again, I have no clue what will happen, and predicting the future is very hard, but let’s take a look at what it would take for that milestone to be achieved.

@singularity7x from Bitwise broke down the financials of Coinbase and extrapolated the metrics needed to drive the ~8x share price increase to reach $1,000. Juan wrote:

- 2023 Net Revenue Estimate: $2.6B
- 2025 Net Revenue Estimate: $14.5B
- 2023 Monthly Transacting Users Estimate: 8.5M
- 2025 Monthly Transacting Users Estimate: 32M
- 2023 Assets on Platform Estimate: $130B
- 2025 Assets on Platform Estimate: $500B

This means that Juan’s analysis concludes that Coinbase’s stock would reach $1,000 if the company hit $14.5 billion in net revenue, 32 million monthly transacting users, and $500 billion of assets on the platform.

Those are a significant increase from the current position, but a strong capital inflow during a bull market is the perfect tailwind to help a company like Coinbase achieve stunning potential growth like this.

As many of you know, I have had financial exposure to Coinbase through the Morgan Creek Digital Assets fund for a number of years. The thesis ~ 5 years ago in the private market was that Coinbase would serve as the best US-based, regulated exchange. They had great brand awareness, the team continued to innovate on the product in an impressive way, and the institutional offerings were starting to serve as the default for large capital allocators as they came into the market.

Each of these components of the thesis still remains true today. Additionally, Coinbase appears to have transitioned a big part of their business into a crypto-native format. According to @QwQiao, “the most impressive thing about Coinbase is they transformed themselves from a CEX to a crypto-native organization: wallet, L2, onchain identity, payment, and more.”

It is not every day that you see a public company rebuild the plane while in flight with such ease. I have no plans to change my personal portfolio based on these recent public comments from various analysts, nor do I ever recommend selling anything in crypto pre-halving, but I am curious to hear what each of you thinks about Coinbase. Here are the income statement, balance sheet, and cash-flow statement.

Reply to this tweet with your thoughts. 

There is great debate in the market around the company, and the market will be the ultimate referee of who is right and wrong.

I look forward to learning from each of you.

@rweisbein @Nasdaq The SEC's view is that bitcoin investment vehicles do not comply with Exchange Act Section 6(b)(5)'s manipulation rule & thus can't trade on public securities exchanges.

If $MSTR traded purely for exposure to bitcoin, you could imagine SEC telling Nasdaq to suspend or delist it.

@BtcPowUpdate @UncleJack123 @Nasdaq I agree, and as long as MicroStrategy keeps running its business, the SEC shouldn't have a problem with how it manages its balance sheet.

I doubt the SEC would take interest unless $MSTR traded primarily as a vehicle for bitcoin exposure, as some people have proposed today.

@Mike_OntheLam @UncleJack123 @Nasdaq @PrestonPysh I'm a fan & long-time listener of @PrestonPysh & have no problem with MicroStrategy. Companies should do what they want with their money.

But lots of people here (including bitcoiners) are acting like $MSTR is a bitcoin ETF & that's not correct.

@PrestonPysh @michael_saylor As a specific use-case example, one of my model portfolio platforms can't buy OTC stocks, which rules out $GBTC and other bitcoin plays in that particular account.

And yet, $MSTR trades on the NASDAQ, so that can go in the account, and did a while back.

@PrestonPysh @michael_saylor Basically making an informal bitcoin ETF.

Any stock fund manager, or institution, or rando investor, can buy $MSTR for indirect bitcoin exposure, and yet remain within their mandate, since ostensibly it's just a normal stock.

No, there isn't a loophole in the federal securities laws allowing a publicly traded company to convert itself into a bitcoin ETF without SEC approval.

The more bitcoin $MSTR buys, the more likely the SEC is to start asking questions that @Nasdaq doesn't want to answer.

@twobitidiot @msantoriESQ @alansilbert @brucefenton @DCGco @awrigh01 @jasonsomensatto @SHodyEsq I fully agree with this. Curious who wrote it?

Also, inadvertent IC status is only one of several problems for $MSTR. If it essentially becomes a commodity ETP, SEC can use the same argument against listing (i.e., against NASDAQ) as for BTC ETFs: manipulation under Sec. 6(b)(5).
